# Generated by ContextEngine — npx @strifero/contextengine
# https://github.com/strifero/ContextEngine

# Project Context

## Stack
- Python 3.11+
- Django 4.x
- PostgreSQL
- Django REST Framework (if detected)
- Celery for background tasks (if detected)

## Architecture
- `<project>/` — Django project package (settings, root URLconf, wsgi/asgi)
- `apps/` — Individual Django apps, one per feature domain
- `templates/` — Project-wide templates; app-level templates inside each app
- `static/` — Project-wide static assets
- `requirements/` — Split requirements files (base.txt, local.txt, production.txt)

## Conventions

### App structure
- One app per feature domain — avoid giant monolithic apps
- Business logic in `services.py` or model methods, not in views
- Keep views thin — they handle HTTP, not business logic
- Use `apps/<name>/` layout with models, views, serializers, urls, admin, tests

### ORM and database
- Use Django ORM for all database access
- Raw SQL only when the ORM cannot express the query — document why
- Use `select_related()` and `prefetch_related()` to avoid N+1 queries
- Define `__str__`, `Meta.ordering`, and `Meta.verbose_name` on all models

### Settings
- Split by environment: `settings/base.py`, `settings/local.py`, `settings/production.py`
- Never commit secrets — use environment variables or django-environ
- Use `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE` to select environment

### URLs
- Include app URLs in project `urls.py` using `include()`
- Use `app_name` for URL namespacing in each app

## What to avoid
- Do not put query logic directly in views
- Do not use `python manage.py` commands in production without confirming migration state
- Do not access `request.user` outside of views — pass it explicitly to services
